<<<KOT:hydrogel>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"hydrogel is a technical material science concept defined by specific polymer chemistry (cross-linked polymer networks with water absorption capacity) and synthetic manufacturing processes. Its identity conditions—the three-dimensional polymer network structure, water content thresholds (≥10%), and biomedical functionality—require domain-specific chemistry vocabulary and concepts (polymers, cross-linking, absorption capacity) not expressible in the prime lexicon or over available catalog concepts. It belongs to the materials science sector, not the semantic primitive tier."}
<<<END:hydrogel>>>>

<<<KOT:idolatrously>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"idolatrously is the adverbial form of idolatrous, which itself denotes a culturally and religiously specific attitude (worship of idols; treating something as an object of worship). The everyday sense presupposes familiarity with religious/cultural practice and the concept of idolatry, which has no stable grounding in the prime lexicon independent of those domain assumptions. The adverbial form compounds this dependency. It cannot be reduced to primitive mental states or actions without loss of sense."}
<<<END:idolatrously>>>>
